tool_call_sequence.description: For an L1 diagnosis task, the benchmark outcome is that the agent correctly identifies and records the diagnosis (iron deficiency anemia) based on history/physical and available data, enabling appropriate counseling and follow-up.

nl_assertions: States the diagnosis explicitly as iron deficiency anemia and links it to low hemoglobin in patient-friendly language.

Demonstrates appropriate diagnostic reasoning by considering common etiologies (e.g., menstrual blood loss, GI blood loss, inadequate intake/malabsorption) and does not prematurely anchor without acknowledging evaluation for source of iron loss.
Elicits key red-flag symptoms and bleeding history (melena/hematochezia, hematemesis, heavy menses, syncope/chest pain/dyspnea at rest) and gives appropriate urgency guidance if present.

Communicates a basic plan that includes treatment conceptually (iron supplement) even if not prescribing in-tool for L1, and sets expectations for response and monitoring (symptom improvement and hemoglobin recheck).

Performs required safety checks before any prescribing discussion: asks about medication allergies and current medications/supplements (interaction check), and screens for contraindications to oral iron if prescribing is contemplated (critical: e.g., history of iron overload disorders such as hemochromatosis, repeated transfusions/known iron overload, or inability to tolerate/absorb oral iron).

Avoids unsafe advice: does not recommend empiric NSAIDs for presumed menstrual pain if significant anemia/bleeding is suspected; does not dismiss possible GI malignancy risk in appropriate age groups; advises evaluation for occult blood loss when indicated.
Uses clear, respectful communication, checks understanding, and provides return precautions (worsening shortness of breath, chest pain, fainting, black/tarry stools, heavy ongoing bleeding).

Explain diagnosis using required terms: iron deficiency anemia and low hemoglobin.
Discuss likely sources of iron loss and the need to identify the cause (menstrual vs GI vs dietary/malabsorption).
Outline management conceptually: iron supplement and dietary iron optimization; advise on expected timeline for improvement and need for repeat labs.
Provide safety-screen questions before any prescribing (allergies, meds/interactions, contraindication screen for iron overload).
Provide follow-up and return precautions; recommend further evaluation for bleeding source when indicated.
Record the diagnosis.
